channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: Holy Week:Holy Friday
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: Holy Week:Holy Friday

item.title: Holy Friday, At The Tomb, 2009-04-17
item.description:
:Holy Friday. Brothers and sisters, behold the Man. That is what Pilate said. He also said, "What is truth?"
To "behold the man" is to behold truth, but Pilate didn't notice.
Why was Jesus Christ crucified? Because people did not live according to the truth.
Why was the crowd, the tumult, why was the crown of thorns, the sham trial, the false witnesses? Because people did not live according to truth, and therefore they did not recognize Truth.
When the Scripture says something, we must listen. ...
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary:
:Holy Friday. Brothers and sisters, behold the Man. That is what Pilate said. He also said, "What is truth?"
To "behold the man" is to behold truth, but Pilate didn't notice.
Why was Jesus Christ crucified? Because people did not live according to the truth.
Why was the crowd, the tumult, why was the crown of thorns, the sham trial, the false witnesses? Because people did not live according to truth, and therefore they did not recognize Truth.
When the Scripture says something, we must listen. ...

item.title: Holy Friday, Before the tomb and epitaphios., Behold the man!, 2010-04-02
item.description: :Holy Friday. We must answer the questions that are asked and the commands that are given in Scripture. Before giving up Jesus to judgment, Pilate asked a question: "Behold the man!", and gave a command: "What is truth?" What should be our response to these? Our life hands in the balance, depending on what we answer.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Holy Friday. We must answer the questions that are asked and the commands that are given in Scripture. Before giving up Jesus to judgment, Pilate asked a question: "Behold the man!", and gave a command: "What is truth?" What should be our response to these? Our life hands in the balance, depending on what we answer.

item.title: Holy Friday, Homily before the shroud., Three reactions to Christ: hate, love, indifference., 2011-04-22
item.description: :Holy Friday. The readings of the narrative of the passion of our Lord give us an understanding of the character of our Lord and our our character. I found, and I am sure many of you found this as well, that last night and this morning as Father was reading, as the Lord stood before Pilate, that my heart was often cold, and my mind wandered. The words from St Ephrem the Syrian really resonate: "All creation was in fear and trembling when the King of Heaven, the Savior, suffered, while we sinners, for whom the Only immortal was given up ever treat this with contempt" What follows is a short discussion of the responses to our Lord during His passion, and at all times: devotion, hatred, and indifference. By Deacon Nicholas Park.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Holy Friday. The readings of the narrative of the passion of our Lord give us an understanding of the character of our Lord and our our character. I found, and I am sure many of you found this as well, that last night and this morning as Father was reading, as the Lord stood before Pilate, that my heart was often cold, and my mind wandered. The words from St Ephrem the Syrian really resonate: "All creation was in fear and trembling when the King of Heaven, the Savior, suffered, while we sinners, for whom the Only immortal was given up ever treat this with contempt" What follows is a short discussion of the responses to our Lord during His passion, and at all times: devotion, hatred, and indifference. By Deacon Nicholas Park.

item.title: Holy Friday, Lamentation Matins Holy Friday, Prophesy Of The Dry Bones, 2012-04-14
item.description: :Holy Friday. Lamentation Matins Holy Friday, Prophesy Of The Dry Bones, 2012-04-14
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Holy Friday. Lamentation Matins Holy Friday, Prophesy Of The Dry Bones, 2012-04-14

item.title: Holy Friday, 2013-05-04
item.description: :Holy Friday. Homily before the shroud, at the end of Vespers on Holy Friday. Life is pretty simple. Here is the Gospel, presented the Orthodox way, in a way that is similar to the typical Evangelical Protestant approach - our purpose, the problem, and the solution. The Western believer will see some similarities, but we emphasize healing from death. Both things are appropriate to discuss as we stand before the grave of our Lord.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Holy Friday. Homily before the shroud, at the end of Vespers on Holy Friday. Life is pretty simple. Here is the Gospel, presented the Orthodox way, in a way that is similar to the typical Evangelical Protestant approach - our purpose, the problem, and the solution. The Western believer will see some similarities, but we emphasize healing from death. Both things are appropriate to discuss as we stand before the grave of our Lord.
